This house (บ้านครอบครัวไทยร่วมสมัย 3) corresponds to the 3rd entry: size 156 sqm (corrected 2026-07-19, was misread 146), pile-foundation price 1,668,000 baht, spread-footing price 1,588,000 baht. confidence_flags: RE-CORRECTED_2026-07-19_by_user_8x_zoom_of_THIS_house's_own_page88_image_-_prints_๒,๑๔๕,๐๐๐/๒,๐๓๕,๐๐๐_(glyph2_=_๑_wavy_flat_head_NOT_๙_loop+tall_spike)_-_reverses_the_2026-07-16_'RESOLVED'_conclusion_which_misread_the_same_image;_3_copies_(houses_02/03/05)_now_agree

warnings: Series #6 shows a spread-footing price HIGHER than its pile-foundation price, which is unusual (normally pile foundation costs more) -- same anomaly independently observed in house #2's extraction of this identical series-wide table. Consistent across two independent reads, so likely a genuine printed figure (or a source-document error), not a transcription mistake.

RE-CORRECTED_2026-07-19 (reversing the 2026-07-16 'RESOLVED' note that stood here): the 2026-07-16 zoom of this page88 image concluded '๒,๙๔๕,๐๐๐' -- an 8x re-zoom with in-sheet reference glyphs (๙๗๒,๐๐๐ / ๑,๙๑๐,๐๐๐ for the ๙ shape) shows the glyph is actually ๑ (wavy flat head, no loop+spike): the row prints ๒,๑๔๕,๐๐๐ / ๒,๐๓๕,๐๐๐. House #2's ORIGINAL read (2,145,000/2,035,000) was right all along and its 2026-07-16 'fix' has been reverted; house #5's copy (หน้า89) independently agrees at 6x. 2,145,000 also fits the series' size-price progression (#9 180sqm = 2,001,000 < #10 224sqm = 2,145,000) whereas 2,945,000 was an unexplained outlier. Also corrected this file's own #1 (1,080,000→1,020,000), #2 (1,560,000→1,460,000), and sizes for #3/#8 (146→156) per the same 8x glyph pass.
Updated schema_note context: this house (แบบ 3) is 156 sqm (not 146) with prices 1,668,000 (เข็ม) / 1,588,000 (แผ่) -- unchanged, both re-verified.
